tolerance.justification: A and B agree closely on the draw (~0.248-0.25) so p_draw is held near pool. They diverge on the size of Spain's favoritism: A gives p_home 0.598 / p_away 0.154 on strong strength differentials (elo_diff 271, ability_diff 0.976), while B's conservative factor netting yields 0.53/0.22. The evidence supports A here: Rangnick himself frames Austria as massive underdogs who would lose seven to nine of ten meetings with Spain (strategic_incentives#2), corroborating the analytic gap. I therefore raise p_home to 0.58 and lower p_away to 0.175, a moderate move toward A tempered by B's legitimate, documented concern over Spain's blunt finishing. On the shootout the forecasts diverge sharply (A 0.826 vs B 0.49); a level match compresses toward a coin flip and Spain's most recent decisive shootout was a loss to Portugal in the 2025 Nations League final (coaching_staff#0), so I trim the lean off the pool toward B to 0.60 while keeping Spain the favorite.

citations.ref: drivers.elo_diff=271.1 and ability_diff=0.976; A's strength differentials point to a larger Spain edge than B's panel netting
